action.reasoning: Observation: Training still fails with ModuleNotFoundError for xgboost despite fully prepared code in model.py (XGBRegressor with MAE objective, hist method, n_est=1000, depth=9, lr=0.05), train.py (full CSV data, FP featurization, eval_set + EarlyStopping callback, val_mae logging to metrics.jsonl per RAB_ITERATION, best.pkl save), and predict.py; best MAE remains 0.2188 from RF (MLP was 0.231). Iter7 curve shows smooth convergence but FP+ensemble limited. Analysis: xgboost was never installed (packages_to_install omitted from all prior JSONs despite being allowed and persisting); XGB with reg:absoluteerror + early_stop on val should beat RF (~0.15-0.18 MAE expected) on this 2048-dim sparse Morgan FP (r=3) tabular regression for QM9 gaps. FP calc (~4-6min) acceptable with 1.78h/6 iters left. No code bugs. Decision: Install xgboost, run current train.py on full data (logs val_mae, saves best.pkl), submit_eval on best.pkl for test MAE + timing before Mordred/3D GNN opts.
